analyses.name: The Bank of New York Mellon
analyses.verdict: Cleanest technical setup in the scan, backed by real earnings acceleration — buy the breakout, trim before the July 15 print.

financials.summary: The world's largest custody bank continued to benefit from higher markets (fee income scales with assets under custody/administration) and higher-for-longer short rates on its deposit book. Net interest income and fee income both grew, expenses stayed disciplined, and the bank kept returning capital via buybacks.
highlights: EPS growth of roughly +42% YoY per the latest trailing figures
Revenue growth of roughly +13% YoY
Continued share buybacks supporting EPS growth on top of operating gains
Assets under custody/administration near record levels on higher equity markets

accomplishments: Continued expansion of digital-assets/tokenization custody services for institutional clients
Ongoing buyback program shrinking share count
Record or near-record levels of assets under custody

journey_52w.summary: BK spent the past year in a steady Stage 2 uptrend, grinding from the low-$90s to the mid-$140s with only shallow pullbacks — the kind of low-volatility grind institutions favor. It's now coiling in its tightest base of the year (3% contraction) just 2.5% under its 52-week high.

milestones.label: Past year
milestones.note: Steady higher-highs/higher-lows uptrend from the low-$90s toward $147

milestones.label: Recent weeks
milestones.note: Formed a 2-contraction VCP tightening to 3%, with the RS line hitting a new high

risks: Earnings July 15 is a binary event after a big run-up
A market-wide selloff hits custody-fee income (AUC/A) directly since it scales with asset prices
catalysts: July 15 earnings — continued NII/fee beat would confirm the breakout
Any capital-markets rebound lifts AUC/A further
watch: Whether the stock can clear $147.29 (52-week high) on volume
critical.valuation: Fwd P/E ~14x — reasonable for a bank compounding EPS at 40%+, not obviously expensive

analyses.name: JPMorgan Chase
analyses.verdict: The largest US bank still setting the pace for the group heading into earnings — technical and TradingView signals both agree.

financials.summary: JPMorgan kept posting record or near-record profitability across its consumer, markets, and investment-banking franchises, with net interest income holding up better than feared and trading/banking fees recovering as capital-markets activity picked up.
highlights: EPS growth of roughly +17% YoY
Revenue growth of roughly +13% YoY
Fortress balance sheet with CET1 well above regulatory minimums
Continued buybacks and a growing dividend

accomplishments: Continued market-share gains in investment banking and trading
Ongoing technology/AI investment across the consumer and markets businesses
Dividend increases alongside buybacks

journey_52w.summary: JPM has been one of the steadiest large-cap uptrends of the past year, climbing from the low-$200s toward the mid-$330s, now forming a 6-contraction VCP just 0.3% off its high.

milestones.label: Past year
milestones.note: Multi-quarter uptrend on resilient NII and a capital-markets recovery

milestones.label: Recent weeks
milestones.note: Tightened into a 2.3% VCP right under the 52-week high

risks: Earnings in 12 days — binary event risk
RSI 70 near the highs leaves less room before a natural pause
catalysts: Q2 earnings — NII trajectory and trading revenue are the key lines to watch
Any Fed rate-path clarity that firms up NII guidance
critical.valuation: Fwd P/E ~14x, in line with its own history despite best-in-class franchise quality

analyses.name: Eli Lilly
analyses.verdict: The best fundamentals in this entire list — GLP-1 franchise (Mounjaro/Zepbound) still compounding at triple-digit growth rates.

financials.summary: Lilly's incretin franchise (tirzepatide, sold as Mounjaro for diabetes and Zepbound for obesity) continued to scale rapidly, driving outsized revenue and earnings growth as manufacturing capacity expanded to meet demand. Non-incretin pharma and pipeline R&D spend remain heavy but are increasingly funded by incretin cash flow.
highlights: Revenue growth of roughly +56% YoY
EPS growth of roughly +170% YoY, reflecting operating leverage on incretin volume
Continued capacity expansion investments to relieve supply constraints
Pipeline readouts across obesity, Alzheimer's and oncology remain active catalysts

accomplishments: Continued global capacity build-out for tirzepatide manufacturing
Ongoing label/indication expansion efforts for Zepbound/Mounjaro
Pipeline progress in next-generation obesity and metabolic-disease compounds

journey_52w.summary: LLY has been one of the strongest large-cap compounders of the past year on relentless GLP-1 demand, climbing steadily with brief pauses around supply-chain and competitive headlines, now just 3% off its 52-week high.

milestones.label: Past year
milestones.note: Steady climb driven by incretin volume growth and capacity expansion

milestones.label: Recent weeks
milestones.note: 5-contraction VCP tightening to 5.4%, holding near the highs

risks: Valuation (fwd P/E ~27x) leaves little room for a GLP-1 competitive or pricing disappointment
Manufacturing/supply constraints have periodically capped upside surprises
High dollar price ($1,192) means wider absolute-dollar stops
catalysts: Continued Zepbound/Mounjaro volume ramp and any new indication approvals
Pipeline readouts in oral GLP-1s and next-gen obesity compounds
watch: Competitive dynamics vs. other GLP-1 entrants
critical.valuation: Premium multiple, but growth (+170% EPS) is outrunning the multiple expansion so far

analyses.name: Advanced Micro Devices
analyses.verdict: The cleanest AI-accelerator exposure in this list right now — several mega-cap tech names screened weak in the same scan.

financials.summary: AMD's data-center GPU and EPYC server-CPU businesses continued to take share, with the MI-series accelerator ramp and continued EPYC server share gains driving outsized revenue growth; gross margins expanded as the mix shifted toward higher-margin data-center product.
highlights: Revenue growth of roughly +38% YoY
EPS growth of roughly +91% YoY on operating leverage and mix shift
Continued data-center GPU (MI-series) revenue ramp
EPYC server CPU share gains against the incumbent

accomplishments: Continued AI-accelerator roadmap execution (MI-series ramp)
Server CPU share gains in the data center
Client/gaming segments stabilizing alongside data-center strength

journey_52w.summary: AMD has been volatile but trending higher over the past year as the market alternately doubted and re-embraced its AI-accelerator roadmap versus the dominant incumbent; it's currently 6.9% off its 52-week high after a recent basing period.

milestones.label: Past year
milestones.note: Volatile uptrend tracking sentiment on its AI-accelerator competitive position

milestones.label: Recent weeks
milestones.note: 5-contraction VCP tightening to 5.8%, still building the base

risks: Semis are the most crowded, highest-beta trade in the market — sharp drawdowns on any AI-capex doubt
Execution against the dominant GPU incumbent remains the central debate
catalysts: Continued MI-series revenue disclosure/ramp updates
Hyperscaler capex commentary each earnings season
watch: Data-center segment revenue growth rate each quarter
critical.valuation: Fwd P/E ~41x — priced for continued share gains, not for a slowdown

analyses.name: Ralph Lauren
analyses.verdict: Premium-brand pricing power showing up in both margins and the stock's relative strength.

financials.summary: Ralph Lauren's 'elevate the brand' strategy continued to pay off — full-price selling and international/China growth drove both revenue and margin expansion, with average unit retail (AUR) increases showing the pricing power to work.
highlights: Revenue growth of roughly +17% YoY
EPS growth of roughly +20% YoY
Continued full-price mix and AUR growth supporting gross margin expansion
International (Europe, Asia) growth outpacing North America

accomplishments: Continued brand-elevation strategy execution (reduced promotional activity)
International store/DTC expansion, notably in China and Europe
Ongoing share buybacks and dividend growth

journey_52w.summary: RL has been a steady outperformer over the past year as the 'affordable luxury' positioning proved resilient even as broader apparel retail was mixed; RS is up 12% over just the last 4 weeks.

milestones.label: Past year
milestones.note: Steady uptrend on full-price mix and international growth

milestones.label: Recent 4 weeks
milestones.note: RS +12% — accelerating relative outperformance into a tight 2.1% VCP

risks: Premium apparel is discretionary — any real consumer pullback hits AUR and volume together
China consumer-spending trends are a swing factor for the growth story
catalysts: Continued AUR/full-price mix gains each quarter
China/international comp growth
watch: Any read-through from other luxury names on China demand
critical.valuation: Fwd P/E ~20x — reasonable for the growth and margin trajectory

analyses.name: Corning
analyses.verdict: Legacy glass/optics manufacturer re-rated as an AI-datacenter infrastructure play — the earnings growth backs it up.

financials.summary: Corning's Optical Communications segment benefited sharply from hyperscaler datacenter buildouts (fiber connectivity for AI clusters), while its other segments (Display, Mobile Consumer Electronics, Life Sciences) contributed more modest, steady growth.
highlights: EPS growth of roughly +139% YoY
Revenue growth of roughly +20% YoY
Optical Communications segment the primary growth driver on AI-datacenter fiber demand
Margin expansion from higher-value optical connectivity mix

accomplishments: Continued ramp of next-generation optical connectivity products for AI datacenters
Capacity investment to meet hyperscaler fiber demand
Diversified base (Display/Mobile/Life Sciences) providing a floor beneath the optical growth

journey_52w.summary: GLW re-rated meaningfully over the past year as investors recognized it as a picks-and-shovels AI infrastructure name rather than a legacy glass manufacturer; it's currently basing about 14% below its 52-week high after the initial re-rating move.

milestones.label: Past year
milestones.note: Re-rated higher as AI-datacenter fiber demand became the dominant growth narrative

milestones.label: Recent weeks
milestones.note: Basing 14% off the high — a 6-contraction VCP, not yet a fresh breakout

risks: Fwd P/E ~52x prices in a lot of continued AI-capex growth — a pause in hyperscaler spending would hit the multiple hard
Historically a lower-growth, cyclical manufacturer — the market's re-rating could reverse if the AI narrative cools
catalysts: Continued hyperscaler capex commentary/orders
Earnings in 26 days
watch: Optical Communications segment growth rate specifically, not just consolidated revenue
critical.valuation: Rich multiple (52x fwd) relative to its own history — this is a growth re-rating, not a value name anymore

analyses.name: CVS Health
analyses.verdict: Turnaround story with earnings growth and a cheap multiple — rare combination in this list.

financials.summary: CVS continued to work through the margin pressure in its Aetna health-insurance segment (medical cost trend) while its pharmacy/retail and Caremark PBM segments provided steadier cash flow; cost-discipline and pricing actions on the insurance side began showing up in improved earnings.
highlights: EPS growth of roughly +63% YoY off a depressed prior-year base
Revenue growth of roughly +6% YoY
Aetna medical-cost-trend management showing early signs of stabilizing
Continued PBM (Caremark) and retail pharmacy cash-flow contribution

accomplishments: Repricing actions in the Medicare Advantage / Aetna insurance book to address medical-cost trend
Continued cost-reduction initiatives across the enterprise
Store footprint optimization in the retail pharmacy segment

journey_52w.summary: CVS bottomed out after a rough stretch tied to elevated medical costs in its insurance business, then trended higher over the past year as repricing and cost actions started to show through in results; it's now at a fresh 52-week high.

milestones.label: Past year
milestones.note: Recovery uptrend from depressed levels as insurance-segment repricing took hold

milestones.label: Recent weeks
milestones.note: 3-contraction VCP tightened to 3.6%, breaking out to new highs

risks: Medical-cost trend in the insurance segment can reaccelerate and erase the earnings recovery
Earnings in 28 days is a real test of whether the repricing gains are durable
catalysts: Continued evidence that Aetna medical-cost trend is stabilizing
Any additional PBM/Caremark contract wins
watch: Medical loss ratio (MLR) trend in quarterly reports
critical.valuation: Fwd P/E ~12.5x — still priced like a turnaround, despite the EPS recovery already underway

analyses.name: Entergy
analyses.verdict: Direct beneficiary of datacenter power demand in its Gulf South service territory — a cleaner thematic utility than most.

financials.summary: Entergy continued to benefit from large-load (datacenter/industrial) interconnection demand in its Louisiana/Mississippi/Arkansas/Texas service territories, supporting above-average rate-base growth versus regulated-utility peers, even as near-term EPS growth remained modest.
highlights: Revenue growth of roughly +12% YoY
EPS growth roughly flat (+1% YoY) — top line running ahead of the bottom line as capex/financing costs are absorbed
Continued large-load/datacenter interconnection agreements supporting multi-year rate-base growth
Regulated-utility earnings visibility via constructive rate cases

accomplishments: New large-load (datacenter/industrial) interconnection agreements in its service territory
Continued regulated rate-base growth investment program
Constructive regulatory outcomes supporting the capex plan

journey_52w.summary: ETR has trended higher over the past year as the market increasingly priced in datacenter-driven load growth as a multi-year rate-base growth tailwind for Gulf South utilities; it's currently basing in a tight 2.4% VCP about 4% off its high.

milestones.label: Past year
milestones.note: Steady re-rating on datacenter/large-load demand supporting long-term rate-base growth

milestones.label: Recent weeks
milestones.note: Very tight 2.4% VCP forming just under the 52-week high

risks: EPS growth (+1%) is lagging revenue (+12%) — financing costs on the capex buildout are a near-term drag
Regulatory/rate-case risk if commissions push back on cost recovery
catalysts: Additional large-load interconnection announcements
Rate-case outcomes in its service territories
watch: Whether EPS growth starts catching up to revenue growth
critical.valuation: Fwd P/E ~22x — a premium to traditional utilities, justified if the load-growth thesis keeps delivering

analyses.name: Terreno Realty
analyses.verdict: Industrial-warehouse REIT with early institutional buying (pocket pivot) ahead of what looks like an imminent breakout.

financials.summary: Terreno owns and operates industrial/logistics real estate concentrated in six major coastal US markets. Same-store NOI growth and continued acquisition activity in supply-constrained infill industrial markets supported FFO growth in line with the broader industrial-REIT recovery.
highlights: EPS growth of roughly +40% YoY
Revenue growth of roughly +13% YoY
Continued acquisition activity in supply-constrained coastal infill industrial markets
Occupancy remained healthy across its portfolio

accomplishments: Continued acquisitions in target coastal infill industrial markets
Portfolio releasing at positive rent spreads as older leases roll to market rates
Balance-sheet discipline supporting continued external growth

journey_52w.summary: TRNO tracked the broader industrial-REIT recovery over the past year, grinding higher with a pocket pivot (a burst of institutional buying volume) just detected today — often an early tell ahead of a formal technical breakout.

milestones.label: Past year
milestones.note: Grinding uptrend in line with the industrial-REIT sector recovery

milestones.label: Today
milestones.note: Pocket pivot detected — a volume signature associated with early institutional accumulation

risks: REITs are rate-sensitive — a hawkish rate surprise would pressure the whole sector, TRNO included
Industrial/logistics demand growth has moderated from the pandemic-era peak
catalysts: Continued positive re-leasing spreads as below-market leases roll
Additional accretive acquisitions in target markets
watch: Rate-case-style sensitivity to Fed policy signals
critical.valuation: Fwd P/E ~38x is rich for a REIT, but reflects premium coastal infill industrial exposure with limited new supply

analyses.name: SpaceX
analyses.verdict: A fundamentals-and-momentum story, not a technical one yet — brand new to public markets with no chart history to lean on.

financials.period: Pre-IPO / newly listed — no public full fiscal year reported yet
financials.summary: SpaceX's business is built on two pillars: Falcon 9/Falcon Heavy/Starship launch services (where reusability has driven launch costs down and cadence up dramatically versus legacy providers) and Starlink, its satellite-broadband constellation, which has scaled into a large recurring-revenue subscription business. As a newly public company there isn't yet a public 10-K to cite line-item financials from.
highlights: Starlink has scaled into a multi-million-subscriber global broadband business with recurring, subscription-style revenue
Falcon 9 reusability has pushed launch cadence and cost efficiency well beyond legacy competitors
Bloomberg reported analyst price targets around its public debut testing a roughly $2.2 trillion valuation

accomplishments: High-cadence Falcon 9 reusable-booster launch operations
Continued Starship development toward full and rapid reusability
Starlink global broadband subscriber growth, including direct-to-cell service expansion

journey_52w.summary: Not applicable in the traditional sense — SPCX has no 52-week public trading history. The relevant 'journey' has been the pre-IPO narrative: years of rising private-market valuations culminating in a public listing where sell-side analysts are now debuting coverage.

milestones.label: IPO debut
milestones.note: Public listing with sell-side analyst coverage beginning; Bloomberg reported targets testing a ~$2.2T valuation

risks: No trading history means no technical signal is reliable yet — TradingView's STRONG_SELL read here is based on just 4 total oscillator signals and should be disregarded, not acted on
A ~$2.2T valuation leaves very little room for execution missteps on Starship or Starlink growth
New-listing volatility can be extreme in both directions in the first weeks/months of trading
catalysts: Initial sell-side analyst notes and price targets as coverage is initiated
Starship flight-test milestones
Starlink subscriber growth disclosures
watch: First 2-3 months of price action to establish a real base/RS/VCP read
Lock-up expiration timing, which often pressures newly-public stocks
critical.valuation: No traditional multiple to anchor to yet given the lack of public financials — treat this purely as a thesis-driven, story/valuation position sized accordingly
